data.title: A certain whale holds a short position of 1232 BTC, with a floating profit of over $24 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a certain whale (0x5D2F...9Bb7) has been continuously shorting BTC for over six months. Its short position of 1,232 BTC (worth $113.27 million) currently has unrealized profits exceeding $24 million. This address's total profits on Hyperliquid have exceeded $51 million, with funding fee income alone exceeding $9.2 million.

data.title: ZachXBT: Over $3.1 million stolen from GANA Payments on BSC
data.description: According to ZachXBT monitoring, the GANA Payment project was exploited on BSC earlier today, resulting in a loss of over $3.1 million. The attacker first transferred 1,140 BNB ($1.04 million) to Tornado Cash on BSC, then bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, depositing 346 ETH ($1.05 million) into Tornado. The attacker currently still holds 346 ETH ($1.046 million) on Ethereum. The stolen address: 0x2e8a8670b734e260cedbc6d5a05532264aae5c380xd10ed57534dc63f2ea9dc0cb0096086f3cc8fa4d ETH address: 0x7a503e3ab9433ebf13afb4f7f1793c25733b3cca

data.title: The largest short position in Hyperliquid ZEC has a floating loss of over $17 million.
data.description: According to Hyperbot data, with the rebound in ZEC prices, the largest short position holder on Hyperliquid (0xd47...1A91) has suffered a floating loss of over $17 million and has already paid nearly $600,000 in funding fees. Currently, this address holds 59,941.71 ZEC short positions, with an opening price of $384 and a liquidation price of approximately $1,031.

data.title: September Nonfarm Payrolls Expected to Show Modest Growth Amid Economic Uncertainty
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the first nonfarm payroll report since the government shutdown is set to be released tonight, with various institutions offering their perspectives.

Rockefeller anticipates an increase of 50,000 in September's nonfarm employment, suggesting a stable job market despite previously weak labor data. Indeed Hiring Lab does not foresee significant changes in the report, expecting the current labor market weakness to persist.

Pantheon Macroeconomics warns that any disappointing data could be amplified due to the six-week data vacuum, potentially exaggerating the negative impact of the nonfarm figures. A Reuters survey also predicts a 50,000 increase, with economists suggesting that August's data was suppressed by seasonal anomalies and may be revised upwards.

Loyola Marymount University notes a clear slowdown in the labor market, with expectations that this trend will continue, keeping the market at a low point without entering a recession. Nationwide predicts an increase of 40,000 to 50,000, indicating that the summer's labor market weakness has extended into the fall, with companies maintaining a stance of neither hiring nor firing.

Credit Agricole forecasts a 55,000 increase in nonfarm employment with an unemployment rate of 4.3%, describing the labor market as cooling but not collapsing, characterized by low hiring and low layoffs.

Standard Chartered expects nonfarm employment data from September to November to be very weak, with seasonal hiring likely to be minimal and layoffs unusually high, potentially swaying Federal Reserve centrists towards rate cuts.

Goldman Sachs projects an 80,000 increase in September's nonfarm employment with a 4.3% unemployment rate, cautioning that risks may lie in the unreleased October data, which is expected to show a decrease of 50,000.

Union Bank forecasts an increase of approximately 40,000, suggesting that market reactions may be muted as more employment information is available from private sector data.

Consulting firm RSM believes that the September data, along with revisions for July and August, will show a slightly better employment outlook than generally expected, though not significantly impressive, as the labor market and the overall U.S. economy continue to struggle.

(Note: The market consensus expects a 50,000 increase in U.S. nonfarm employment for September, with an unemployment rate of 4.3%.)

data.title: Doppel raises $70 million in Series C funding, led by Bessemer Venture Partners.
data.description: AI-driven brand protection platform Doppel has raised $70 million in Series C funding, led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with participation from a16z, South Park Commons, Script Capital, 9Yards Capital, Sozo Ventures, Strategic Cyber ​​Ventures, CrowdStrike CEO George Kurtz, NTT DOCOMO Ventures, Aurum Partners, and WNBA players Nneka Ogwumike, Breanna Stewart, and Kelsey Plum.

data.title: India's Debt-Backed ARC Token Eyes Tentative Q1 2026 Debut, Sources Say
data.description:
According to sources, it has been reported that India's innovative debt-backed ARC token is aiming for a tentative launch in the first quarter of 2026. This development could mark a significant step in the evolution of digital assets within the country, as stakeholders and industry experts closely monitor the progress and regulatory implications surrounding this emerging financial instrument.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/markets/2025/11/20/india-s-debt-backed-arc-token-eyes-tentative-january-2026-debut-sources-say

data.title: On Polymarket, over $5.37 million has been wagered on the "Monad public offering fundraising total" event, with an estimated 81% probability of betting on "the public offering raising over $300 million."
data.description: According to Polymarket, over $5.37 million has been wagered on the "Monad IPO fundraising total." The probabilities are as follows: 81% for betting on a total fundraising exceeding $300 million; 59% for exceeding $400 million; 26% for exceeding $600 million; 13% for exceeding $800 million; 7% for exceeding $1 billion; and 4%, 3%, and 1% for exceeding $1.2 billion or more. It is worth noting that all these probabilities have decreased to varying degrees.

data.title: Monad's public offering has raised over $140 million, achieving 76.2% of its total goal.
data.description: According to data shared by crypto KOL Swishi.eth, the public sale of Monad (MON) tokens on Coinbase has raised $142.9 million, achieving 76.2% of its total target. At the current fundraising pace, a conservative estimate puts the final amount at $181 million; an optimistic estimate suggests it could exceed $240 million. This public sale offered 7.5% of the total MON token supply at a price of 0.025 USDC, with a FDV of $2.5 billion, and closed on November 23rd at 10:00 AM.
